name: Chili Beef Tacos
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 lb Stewing beef cut into 1/2 in cubes
28 oz Italian plum tomatoes, drained and crushed
10 oz Pkg. frozen corn, thawed and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix chili powder, garlic, lime juice, 3 tbsp olive oil, and cumin to form a paste.
2. Add the beef and stir until coated. Refrigerate for 24 hours, stirring occasionally.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F. Combine marinated beef, canned tomatoes, broth, and beer in a Dutch oven.
4. Bring mixture to boil over high heat. Cover and transfer to oven; bake for 45 minutes.
5. Uncover and continue baking until beef is tender (about 45 more minutes). Let cool, shred beef, and return to cooking liquid. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
6. Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a heavy skillet over medium-low heat. Add onion and jalapenos; cover and cook until onion is tender and lightly browned, stirring occasionally (about 15 minutes).
7. Strain beef cooking liquid into the skillet. Bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer uncovered until sauce thickens and reduces to 1/2 cup, stirring occasionally (about 50 minutes).
8. Mix in shredded beef. This filling can be prepared up to one day in advance and refrigerated.
9. Add corn, olives, and pimentos; stir over medium heat until heated through (about 5 minutes). Season with salt and pepper to taste.
10. Assemble tacos by filling shells half full with the beef mixture. Top with cheese, lettuce, and chopped tomatoes. Serve immediately, passing salsa and sour cream separately.
Note: The same filling works well in chimichangas and enchiladas and is delicious when spooned over toasted hamburger rolls. It can also be frozen.
